title: Workhorse Group Inc (WKHS) Q4 2024 Earnings Call Highlights
text:
## Financial Performance
Workhorse reported declining revenues of $6.6 million for 2024, down sharply from $13.1 million in 2023. The company posted a net loss of $101.8 million, though improved from prior year's $123.9 million loss. Operating expenses decreased with SG&A at $42.5 million and R&D at $9.1 million. Year-end cash: $4.6 million with $41.8 million inventory. Interest expenses surged to $22.2 million.
## Product Development
The company launched the W56 208-inch wheelbase truck with positive customer feedback and received purchase order for 13 units. Workhorse secured approval to sell W750 and W56 models in Canada, expanding geographic footprint. CEO Dauch noted the company established national dealer network and service infrastructure.
## Operational Challenges
Commercial EV sector faced regulatory headwinds. Recent regulatory pauses created market uncertainty including California EPA waiver withdrawal and federal procurement freezes. Sales declined due to lower W4 CC volumes. Company executed reverse stock split to maintain NASDAQ compliance.
## Strategic Focus
Management emphasized cost reductions, bringing monthly operating cash below $3 million. Company continues pursuing fleet orders from FedEx contractors under 3-year master framework agreement.
